# QA Plan: Sprint 9 — Polish Sprint (Pre-Production → Production gate-clearing)

**Date**: 2026-04-28
**Generated by**: /qa-plan
**Scope**: 14 stories (5 Must Have + 3 Should Have + 6 Nice to Have)
**Engine**: Godot 4.6 (mono build verified via headless test runner)
**Sprint File**: `production/sprints/sprint-9.md`
**Sprint goal**: Clear the Pre-Production → Production gate via 3 minimal-path polish tickets + high-leverage backlog overflow

---

## Sprint 9 testing context

This is a **polish sprint**, not a feature sprint. The primary risk is **regression** in the 49/49 Sprint 8 UI integration suites + ~1116 project-wide tests. Every Sprint 9 story should:

1. Land changes that pass the existing test suite without modification (or with documented test updates if behavior contract genuinely changes)
2. Be guarded by new test cases proportionate to the surface modified
3. Be verified manually for the felt-experience criteria that define "gate-clearing" — Pillar 2 score ≥3/5 (was 1/5), zero unprompted confusion statements

**Definition of Sprint 9 success per gate**: `/gate-check production` retry returns PASS or CONCERNS (not FAIL). All testing prioritization should serve that single metric.

---

| Story | Type | Automated Test Required | Manual Verification Required |
|-------|------|------------------------|------------------------------|
| **S9-M1** formation_assignment UX polish | UI (primary) + Logic (slot active-state) | Unit test — slot active-state setter; integration test extension to existing `formation_assignment_screen_test.gd` for new affordance behavior | Manual walkthrough — slot affordance visible on tap; instructional copy renders; floor card readable. **Fresh-eyes playtest sign-off (S9-M4)**. |
| **S9-M2** run pacing minimum-perceived-duration | Visual/Feel + Config/Data (DWELL_MS const change) | Unit test — `RUN_END_DWELL_MS` constant in valid [0, 2000] range (extended from prior [0, 350]); integration test for dwell-honored auto-route timing | **Smoke check** — 5+ test dispatches, no run completes in <2s wall-clock |
| **S9-M3** locale CSV authoring | Config/Data | Smoke check — `tr()` returns non-key strings; lint test for the 12 expected keys | Manual walkthrough — every visible UI string is real English, not a locale key |
| **S9-M4** fresh-eyes playtest with polish applied | Playtest | None (manual session) | **Playtest report at `production/playtests/playtest-04-post-sprint-9-polish-2026-XX-XX.md`**. Pillar 2 score ≥3/5 + zero unprompted confusion + run pacing reported "watchable". |
| **S9-M5** /gate-check production retry | Process | None (gate-check skill verifies artifacts) | **Gate-check report at `production/gate-checks/2026-XX-XX-pre-production-to-production.md`** |
| **S9-S1** Save-persist pipeline end-to-end | Integration (highest-priority Should Have) | Integration test — full save→close→reload→state-restored cycle across orchestrator + hero-roster + economy; unit tests on SaveLoadSystem.persist file-write path | Manual walkthrough — verify `~/Library/Application Support/Godot/app_userdata/Lantern Guild/save*` file exists after a complete dispatch cycle. |
| **S9-S2** scene-manager Story 009 (reduce_motion + offline-replay cozy modal + return_to_app) | Integration + UI | Integration test — `reduce_motion` clamps transitions to 50ms; cozy-modal time-gated at ≥100ms estimated replay; return_to_app screen routes correctly on offline-gain detection | Manual walkthrough — reduce_motion toggle works; cozy modal renders during chunked offline replay |
| **S9-S3** parchment_theme.tres content authoring | Visual/Feel + Config/Data | None (no logic) | Manual visual verification — palette matches Art Bible §4; typography matches §7; theme cascade visible across all screens |
| **S9-N1** scene_manager test env flakes cleanup | Integration (test infrastructure) | Existing tests must transition from FAIL to PASS — no new test files; modify `modal_pause_tick_coupling_test.gd`, `crossfade_timing_test.gd`, `request_screen_and_node_swap_test.gd` to address Godot 4.6.1 mono+headless quirks | Smoke check — test suite returns full PASS, not flake-skipped |
| **S9-N2** Cross-test live-autoload contamination cleanup | Integration (test infrastructure) | Modify `tests/unit/dungeon_run_orchestrator/autoload_skeleton_and_di_test.gd` to add before_test/after_test snapshot+restore; no new test functions, just isolation hardening | Test suite — autoload_skeleton_and_di_test.gd passes 17/17 when run alongside other suites (currently fails 2/17 in that scenario) |
| **S9-N3** tr() safe-format helper hoisting | Logic | Unit test — `UIFramework.format_localized(key, value)` handles both "with-placeholder" and "key-as-fallback" cases; existing safe-format guards in Stories 011/012 refactored to call helper; their tests still pass | None (refactor; no UX change) |
| **S9-N4** Re-dispatch shortcut on main_menu | UI | Integration test — main_menu's "Re-dispatch" button (if added) navigates directly to formation_assignment | Manual walkthrough — button visible + functional |
| **S9-N5** XP/level grant feedback | Logic + UI | Unit test — orchestrator emits `hero_leveled` signal at appropriate threshold (verify orchestrator emits this currently or add the emit); integration test for UI toast surfacing | Manual walkthrough — Theron levels up after sufficient dispatches; toast appears |
| **S9-N6** UIFramework completion (apply_parchment_panel, wire_touch_feedback) | Logic + UI | Unit tests for the 2 new static helper methods; integration test for theme variation application + touch-feedback tween lifecycle | Manual visual verification — panels render with parchment styling; touch pulse fires on Button press |

## Automated Tests Required

### S9-M1 — formation_assignment UX polish (UI + Logic)

**Test file path**: extension to existing `tests/integration/scene_manager/formation_assignment_screen_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- Slot active-state visual indicator: tap slot N → `_active_slot_index == N` AND visual state observable (e.g., a `theme_type_variation == "ActiveSlot"` or `modulate.a` change OR a child Border node visibility)
- Instructional header copy renders (label has non-empty text after locale CSV applied per S9-M3)
- Floor context card content (if implemented) — name + difficulty + reward text non-empty
- Existing 13 tests still pass (no regression on AC-1 through AC-7)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Tapping slot N twice — same active_slot_index, no error
- Tapping all 3 slots in sequence — last tapped is the active one
- Tapping slot 0, then tapping slot 0 again — idempotent

**Estimated test count**: ~3-5 new unit/integration tests in addition to existing 13

### S9-M2 — Run pacing minimum-perceived-duration (Visual/Feel + Config/Data)

**Test file path**: extension to `tests/integration/scene_manager/run_end_to_main_menu_transition_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `RUN_END_DWELL_MS` constant value updated (current: 0; target: 1500-2000 if dwell path chosen)
- Test renamed from `..._dwell_constant_is_in_valid_range` to allow new range; assert in `[0, 2000]`
- Auto-route timing test honors the new dwell — total elapsed RUN_ENDED→main_menu IDLE: now expected ≥`RUN_END_DWELL_MS` ms (was ≤500ms)
- Per Story 013 §AC-3 spec deviation note required if RUN_END_DWELL_MS > 350

**Edge cases to cover**:
- DWELL_MS = 0 — no await, route fires same frame (preserve old test path as default-path branch)
- DWELL_MS > 0 — `await get_tree().create_timer(...)` honored; route fires after dwell

**Estimated test count**: ~2 modified tests + 1 new test

### S9-M3 — Locale CSV authoring (Config/Data)

**Test file path**: `tests/integration/locale/en_csv_loaded_test.gd` (new file)
**What to test**:
- The 12 expected keys all resolve via `tr()` to non-key (i.e., real English) strings:
  - `formation_assignment_title` → "Formation"
  - `slot_empty_label` → "Empty"
  - `dispatch_button` → "Dispatch"
  - `dispatch_error_empty_formation` → "Assign at least one hero to your formation."
  - `dispatch_error_floor_locked` → "That floor is locked."
  - `dispatch_error_generic` → "Could not start the dispatch."
  - `recruit_a_hero_label` → "Recruit a hero to begin."
  - `floor_label_forest_reach_1` → "Forest Reach — Floor 1"
  - `dungeon_run_view_title` → "Dungeon Run"
  - `tick_label_prefix` → "Tick:"
  - `kill_count_label_prefix` → "Kills:"
  - `run_complete_kill_count_format` → "Run Complete — %d kills"
- For each key: `assert_str(tr(key)).is_not_equal(key)` — confirms locale CSV loaded

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Missing key — `tr("missing_key")` returns the key (Godot default; not a bug, just contract verification)
- Key with format placeholder — `tr("run_complete_kill_count_format") % 7` produces "Run Complete — 7 kills"

**Estimated test count**: ~12 unit tests (1 per key) + 2 edge-case tests

### S9-S1 — Save-persist pipeline end-to-end (Integration — highest priority Should Have)

**Test file path**: `tests/integration/save_load/persist_pipeline_end_to_end_test.gd` (new file)
**What to test**:
- Full save→close→reload→state-restored cycle:
  - Setup: dispatch a run, advance a few ticks, trigger heartbeat persist OR scene_boundary_persist
  - Verify: `user://save.dat` (or schema path) exists after persist
  - Verify: file content is non-empty and parseable (CBOR or whatever ADR-0004 decided)
  - Verify: closing + reopening the autoload chain restores HeroRoster._heroes, formation_slots, current_orchestrator_state, kill_count
- TickSystem heartbeat persist — every 60s while ACTIVE_FOREGROUND, persist fires (ADR-0005)
- scene_boundary_persist receiver — when SceneManager fires the signal before dungeon_run_view, SaveLoadSystem.persist runs and writes file

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Persist during graceful exit (NOTIFICATION_WM_CLOSE_REQUEST) — file written before app closes
- Persist failure (disk full simulated) — emits save_failed signal, no partial write
- Save file corruption on load — falls back to first-launch (per ADR-0004 anti-tamper)

**Estimated test count**: ~6-8 integration tests

### S9-S2 — scene-manager Story 009 (Integration + UI)

**Test file path**: `tests/integration/scene_manager/reduce_motion_and_offline_replay_modal_test.gd` (new file)
**What to test**:
- `reduce_motion` flag setter writes to `user://settings.cfg` AND clamps subsequent transitions to 50ms (TR-scene-manager-027)
- CEREMONY transition replaced with instant cut when `reduce_motion == true` (TR-scene-manager-036)
- Cozy modal threshold gate — `PROGRESS_MODAL_THRESHOLD_MS = 100` per ADR-0014 §5; modal shown only if estimated replay >100ms
- Modal auto-dismisses on `offline_rewards_collected` signal
- return_to_app screen route fires on offline-gain detection (TR-scene-manager-039)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- reduce_motion toggled off mid-session — subsequent transitions revert to full timing
- Offline replay <100ms — modal not shown (silent fast resolution)
- Offline replay exactly at threshold — boundary behavior verified

**Estimated test count**: ~5-7 integration tests

### S9-N1 — scene_manager test env flakes cleanup (Integration test infrastructure)

**Test file path**: existing files modified, no new files
**What to test (via fixes, not new tests)**:
- `modal_pause_tick_coupling_test.gd` 5 tests: investigate Godot 4.6.1 mono headless quirk; likely needs SceneManager state isolation pattern (same as S8-M4 test isolation work)
- `crossfade_timing_test.gd` 2 tests: investigate Tween + headless interaction; may need to skip-with-justification or adapt timing tolerance
- `request_screen_and_node_swap_test.gd` multiple tests: investigate first-launch routing race condition (similar to Story 011 test pattern)

**Acceptance**: each file goes from FAIL → PASS in headless test runner. No new test functions; existing test functions adjusted for environment-resilience.

### S9-N2 — Cross-test live-autoload contamination cleanup (Integration test infrastructure)

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/dungeon_run_orchestrator/autoload_skeleton_and_di_test.gd` (modify in place)
**What to test (via fixes)**:
- Add `before_test()`/`after_test()` snapshot+restore for `DungeonRunOrchestrator.state`, `run_snapshot`, `_last_dispatch_ms`, `_dispatched_floor_index`, `_dispatched_biome_id`
- Same pattern as `dungeon_run_view_screen_test.gd` already uses

**Acceptance**: 17/17 PASS when run alongside `tests/unit/dungeon_run_orchestrator/state_changed_signal_test.gd` and other orchestrator suites (currently 15/17 with 2 fails on `test_orchestrator_state_initial_value_is_no_run` and `test_orchestrator_run_snapshot_initial_value_is_null`).

### S9-N3 — tr() safe-format helper hoisting (Logic refactor)

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/ui_framework/format_localized_test.gd` (new file)
**What to test**:
- `UIFramework.format_localized("run_complete_kill_count_format", 7)`:
  - When CSV resolves with `%d` placeholder → "Run Complete — 7 kills"
  - When CSV missing (key returned verbatim) → "run_complete_kill_count_format 7" (fallback append)
- Idempotent for keys with no placeholder — `format_localized("plain_key", 7)` → "plain_key 7"
- Existing Story 011 `_show_toast` and Story 012 `_show_run_end_overlay` refactored to call `format_localized` — their tests still pass (regression check)

**Estimated test count**: ~4-5 unit tests + 0 modified existing tests

### S9-N5 — XP/level grant feedback (Logic + UI)

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/dungeon_run_orchestrator/xp_grant_test.gd` OR `tests/unit/hero_roster/level_up_signal_test.gd` (new file, location TBD by where XP-grant logic lives)
**What to test (gated on existing implementation)**:
- Investigation phase: does `dungeon_run_orchestrator.gd` currently grant XP per kill? If not, Sprint 9 may need to defer this to Sprint 10 or scope to "wire the grant logic" first.
- If grant exists: `hero_leveled(instance_id, old_level, new_level)` signal emits at appropriate threshold
- UI integration test: dungeon_run_view or main_menu surface a `hero_leveled` toast on reception

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Multiple level-ups in a single run (should emit one signal per level transition)
- Level cap reached — no further emission
- Hero removed mid-run — no orphaned signal

**Estimated test count**: ~3-5 tests (gated on whether grant logic exists)

### S9-N6 — UIFramework completion (Logic + UI)

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/ui_framework/apply_parchment_panel_test.gd` and `tests/unit/ui_framework/wire_touch_feedback_test.gd` (new files)
**What to test**:
- `apply_parchment_panel(panel, pattern)` sets `theme_type_variation = "ParchmentPanel"`; for `pattern == DECORATIVE` sets `mouse_filter = PASS`; for `pattern == STANDARD` no mouse_filter override (theme variation default applies)
- `wire_touch_feedback(control)` connects pressed/touch event to a 1.05× scale tween of 80ms with 1-frame return; tween-on-PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS for parent

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Apply to a non-PanelContainer Control — should still set theme_type_variation but log a push_warning
- Wire touch feedback to a non-Button Control — should log push_warning, gracefully skip

**Estimated test count**: ~3-4 unit tests per helper (~6-8 total)

## Manual QA Checklist

### S9-M1 — formation_assignment UX polish

**Verification method**: Manual step-through + fresh-eyes playtest sign-off (S9-M4)
**Who must sign off**: Project lead (solo mode); ideally external playtester for fresh-eyes
**Evidence to capture**: Screenshot of formation_assignment with the new affordances + a verbatim quote from a fresh-eyes tester confirming they understood what to do

Checklist:
- [ ] Tapping a slot button visually changes the slot (color, border, "Selected" text — pick one and verify visible to the eye)
- [ ] After tapping a slot, tapping a hero in the Roster panel moves the hero into that slot (data layer was already correct; verify the visual feedback now confirms this)
- [ ] Header text "Send your guild to:" (or similar) renders above the floor button
- [ ] Floor button has surrounding context — name + brief description / enemy preview / reward indicator
- [ ] All 13 existing formation_assignment_screen_test tests still pass

### S9-M2 — Run pacing minimum-perceived-duration

**Verification method**: Smoke check (5+ dispatches; measure wall-clock time)
**Who must sign off**: Project lead
**Evidence to capture**: Screenshot of dungeon_run_view with tick + kill_count animating up; timing log showing run duration ≥2s wall-clock

Checklist:
- [ ] Dispatch 5+ times; record wall-clock duration of each run
- [ ] No run completes (overlay → main_menu) in <2 seconds
- [ ] Player consistently registers the kill_count before the auto-route fires
- [ ] If using DWELL_MS path: existing test `..._dwell_constant_is_in_valid_range` updated to allow new range
- [ ] If using combat-tuning path: combat-resolution.md GDD updated with new tick-budget targets

### S9-M3 — Locale CSV authoring

**Verification method**: Manual walkthrough of every screen + smoke check
**Who must sign off**: Project lead
**Evidence to capture**: Screenshots of each screen showing real English text (not locale keys)

Checklist:
- [ ] guild_hall (placeholder text or future content) — keys resolve
- [ ] formation_assignment — every visible label is English
- [ ] dungeon_run_view — tick + kill_count prefixes are "Tick:" / "Kills:" not keys
- [ ] dungeon_run_view run-end overlay — "Run Complete — N kills" format renders correctly
- [ ] main_menu (placeholder) — keys resolve
- [ ] CSV file at `assets/locale/en.csv` (or schema path) committed
- [ ] project.godot `internationalization/locale/translations` registered

### S9-M4 — Fresh-eyes playtest with polish applied

**Verification method**: Single playtest session, fresh-eyes lens
**Who must sign off**: Project lead (or external playtester if available)
**Evidence to capture**: Playtest report at `production/playtests/playtest-04-post-sprint-9-polish-2026-XX-XX.md`

Checklist:
- [ ] Tester completes Guild Hall → Dispatch → Run → MainMenu loop **without external explanation**
- [ ] Pillar 2 score ≥3/5 (was 1/5 in S8-M5)
- [ ] Run pacing reported as "watchable" — tester registers the run, not just the screen swap
- [ ] **ZERO unprompted confusion statements** about what to do on formation_assignment (was 4 in S8-M5)
- [ ] At least 1 unprompted positive statement (e.g., "oh, I see, I'm sending Theron to fight!")
- [ ] Pillar 1 score ≥3/5 (was 2/5)

### S9-M5 — /gate-check production retry

**Verification method**: gate-check skill
**Who must sign off**: gate-check skill output verdict
**Evidence to capture**: Gate-check report at `production/gate-checks/2026-XX-XX-pre-production-to-production.md`

Checklist:
- [ ] All 13 required artifacts still present
- [ ] All 9 quality checks passing
- [ ] **VS Validation 4/4** (was 2/1/1 — 2 PASS, 1 FAIL, 1 BORDERLINE)
- [ ] Verdict: **PASS** or **CONCERNS** (not FAIL)
- [ ] On PASS: `production/stage.txt` updated to `Production`

### S9-S1 — Save-persist pipeline end-to-end

**Verification method**: Manual walkthrough + integration test
**Who must sign off**: Project lead
**Evidence to capture**: `ls ~/Library/Application Support/Godot/app_userdata/Lantern Guild/` showing save file present after a complete dispatch cycle

Checklist:
- [ ] Save file appears in user://save.dat (or schema path) after first dispatch's scene_boundary_persist
- [ ] Save file survives full app close + relaunch
- [ ] On relaunch: HeroRoster._heroes restored (Theron present); formation_slots restored; orchestrator state restored
- [ ] TickSystem heartbeat (every 60s) verified writing to save file (check file mtime advances)
- [ ] Save schema matches ADR-0004

### S9-S3 — parchment_theme.tres content authoring

**Verification method**: Manual visual comparison against Art Bible §4 + §7
**Who must sign off**: Art director (solo mode = project lead) + design team review
**Evidence to capture**: Screenshots of every screen with the parchment theme applied

Checklist:
- [ ] Palette matches Art Bible §4 — parchment cream, slate ink, lantern amber, dusk purple
- [ ] Typography matches §7 — info_font (≥16px body) + identity_font (≥24px headers, sparingly)
- [ ] No hardcoded `Color(...)` literals in any UI code (CI grep verifies)
- [ ] Theme cascade visible on all screens (single canonical theme + variations)
- [ ] Hover state renders on Buttons (mouse-focus path)
- [ ] No keyboard-focus visuals (FOCUS_NONE per single-focus-mode strategy)

## Smoke Test Scope

Critical paths to verify before any QA hand-off for Sprint 9:

1. **Cold-launch boots cleanly** to guild_hall (placeholder); no crash, no error in Output panel
2. **Theron's first-launch seed** still fires (HeroRoster autoload-gate guard from S8-M4 hotfix #3 intact)
3. **Guild Hall → "Go to Dispatch" → formation_assignment** routes correctly; CROSS_FADE 150ms timing intact
4. **formation_assignment Dispatch → orchestrator.dispatch → state_changed → request_screen("dungeon_run_view")** chain intact (S8-M4 hotfix #4 intact)
5. **Run completes during transition → on_enter defensive route → main_menu** chain intact (S8-M4 hotfix #5 intact)
6. **MainRoot Fade ColorRect mouse_filter = IGNORE** intact (S8-M4 hotfix #2 — clicks must still pass through)
7. **Existing 49/49 Sprint 8 UI integration suite + ~1116 project-wide tests** still PASS — no regressions from Sprint 9 work
8. **Performance**: no new frame spikes during the polish surfaces (esp. theme cascade, locale CSV load)
9. **Save file** at `~/Library/Application Support/Godot/app_userdata/Lantern Guild/` (after S9-S1) — exists and is non-empty after a dispatch
10. **No hardcoded `Color(r,g,b)` literals in UI code** (after S9-S3 parchment theme — code grep test)

*Smoke tests are verified by the developer via `/smoke-check sprint`. Reference this list when running that skill.*

---

## Playtest Requirements

| Story | Playtest Goal | Min Sessions | Target Player Type |
|-------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|
| S9-M4 | Verify the 3 polish tickets (M1/M2/M3) collectively resolved the S8-M5 confusion + pacing findings; confirm Pillar 2 score ≥3/5 + zero unprompted confusion statements | 1 | New player (or deliberate fresh-eyes simulation by project lead) |
| S9-S2 (if implemented) | Verify return_to_app cozy modal feels appropriate for offline-gain reentry — not jarring, not patronizing | 1 (or fold into S9-M4) | Returning player simulation |

**Sign-off requirement**: Playtest notes must be written to `production/playtests/playtest-04-post-sprint-9-polish-2026-XX-XX.md` and reviewed by project lead before S9-M5 gate-check fires.

If S9-S2 implementation slips (Should Have, depends on S9-S1 save-persist working), the offline-replay playtest folds into Sprint 10. Don't block Sprint 9 gate-clearing on it.

---

## Definition of Done — Sprint 9

A story is DONE when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified — via automated test result OR documented manual evidence (screenshot, video, or playtest notes with sign-off)
- [ ] Test file exists at the specified path for all Logic and Integration stories
- [ ] Manual evidence document exists for all Visual/Feel and UI stories (including S9-M1 evidence file under `production/qa/evidence/`)
- [ ] Smoke check passes (`/smoke-check sprint`) before the final gate-check fires
- [ ] No regressions on 49/49 Sprint 8 UI suites + ~1116 project-wide tests
- [ ] Code reviewed (`/code-review` per Sprint 6/7/8 cadence — solo mode skips LP-CODE-REVIEW gate but inline review still runs)
- [ ] Story file updated to `Status: Complete` (via `/story-done`)

**Sprint 9 gate-clearing criteria** (specific to Sprint 9 success metric):
- [ ] S9-M5 `/gate-check production` retry returns **PASS** or **CONCERNS** (not FAIL)
- [ ] `production/stage.txt` advances from `Pre-Production` to `Production`

---

## Risk Register (for QA team awareness)

1. **Polish work may introduce subtle regressions** — adding active-state visuals to formation_assignment slots could break the existing slot button signal connections. Mitigation: extend existing test suite first, then add affordance, then re-run.
2. **Locale CSV wiring may surface project.godot config issues** — Godot's translation system has post-cutoff quirks. Mitigation: test with `tr()` for every key in unit suite before declaring S9-M3 done.
3. **Save-persist surface may be larger than 2 days** — investigation alone could take a day. Mitigation: defer offline computation work to Sprint 10 if the basic save/load round-trip already takes the budget.
4. **Re-playtest may surface NEW confusion not present in S8-M5** — applying polish might fix old issues but reveal new ones. Mitigation: treat as expected; document new findings as Sprint 10 backlog rather than blocking gate.
